text.title: Saga Pattern for Distributed Transactions Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure for designing and shipping a saga.

step.name: model-steps-and-comps
step.action: List ordered (service, action, compensation) triples. For each step, define the local transaction + paired compensation. Mark idempotency mechanism per step.
step.decision-gate: If any step has no compensation, STOP — the saga is not safe.

step.name: choose-coordination
step.action: Choreography for ≤3 steps + 1 team OR if event-driven culture is strong. Orchestration for >3 steps OR cross-team OR debugging-heavy domain.

step.name: wire-outbox
step.action: Add `outbox` table to each service. Wrap step write + outbox insert in one local DB transaction. Wire Debezium CDC OR explicit outbox-poller publishing to Kafka.
step.decision-gate: If any step still does dual-write (db.save + broker.publish in code), STOP and refactor to outbox.

step.name: implement-orchestrator-or-events
step.action: Orchestration: write Temporal/Camunda workflow per saga; each step is an activity; failure triggers reverse-order compensations. Choreography: each service subscribes to predecessor's event, handles failure by publishing rollback event.

step.name: chaos-test-and-document
step.action: Inject failure at each step; verify all preceding compensations execute in reverse order. Verify duplicated events produce no double-effect. Document saga in /docs/sagas/place-order.md.
step.output: Chaos test green + saga doc committed.
